linux-gfx90a.blocked_reason: revalidate at fork 2378586 (head advanced by the gfx1100 delta-port). The two delta fixes are arch-unified and live in HIP-backend-only files (src/backend/hip/math.hpp, src/backend/hip/nvrtc_shims/cuComplex.h) plus a runtime-guarded test eps in test/cholesky_dense.cpp; the CUDA backend is byte-for-byte unchanged. The where complex ==/!= ADL fix is a genuine fix for gfx90a too (the where cfloat JIT compile was failing overload resolution on gfx90a as well), so this revalidate is expected to come back 126/132 with where now passing. Previously completed at 86fbbbe (126/132).

linux-gfx1100.blocked_reason: delta-ported at fork 2378586 (porter fixed the 2 gfx1100 follower failures). Both root causes turned out NOT to be the gfx1100-only COMGR bug the validator hypothesized: (1) where cfloat/cdouble AF_ERR_INTERNAL was a host-set C++ name-lookup bug, NOT a COMGR codegen crash and NOT arch-specific -- a standalone hiprtc repro shows the scan_first<cuFloatComplex,uint,af_notzero_t,...> JIT compile fails overload resolution IDENTICALLY on gfx90a AND gfx1100 (the COMGR "Failing to compile to realloc" message merely wraps that compile error). Root cause: the JIT POD cuFloatComplex/cuDoubleComplex live in the GLOBAL namespace but arrayfire complex ==/!= were in namespace arrayfire::cuda (math.hpp), unreachable by ADL from common::Transform. Fix is ARCH-UNIFIED (correct on both waves): cuComplex.h shim defines the complex ==/!= in the global namespace beside the POD; math.hpp drops its arrayfire::cuda complex ==/!= on the RTC path to avoid ambiguity. CUDA backend untouched (separate files). (2) cholesky_dense cfloat large is genuine RDNA3 FP32 accumulation drift (verified: recovered factor matches a double reference to FP32 precision, relative factor error ~3e-9; the 0.073 is the matmul reconstruction over a 1024-len complex dot product) -- test/cholesky_dense.cpp widens ONLY the cfloat large eps to 0.1 on the RDNA HIP backend (runtime compute-major check), gfx90a/CUDA keep 0.05. Local gfx1100 (W7800) full CUDA.* ctest = 126/132, the SAME 6 documented residuals (blas schar, confidence_connected, sparse x3, threading), NO new failures. where 56/56 (was 54/56), cholesky_dense 32/32 (was 30/32); where x2 + scan x2 deterministic; scan/scan_by_key/reduce/ireduce/convolve/approx/complex unchanged. Next: reviewer. See notes.md gfx1100 delta-port entry.
